problem: A researcher wants to model heat diffusion along a one-dimensional rod over a fixed time interval. The thermal diffusivity is known, the initial temperature profile is provided, and both ends of the rod are held at prescribed fixed temperatures. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow to approximate the temperature field.

problem: A scientist needs to approximate the displacement of a one-dimensional vibrating string. The wave speed is known, both the initial displacement and initial velocity are provided, and the endpoint boundary conditions are fixed. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow for the displacement field.

source_provenance.note: Adapted as a natural-language planning task; no numerical data copied.

problem: A team wants to model a one-dimensional viscous Burgers system on a periodic spatial domain. The initial velocity profile and viscosity coefficient are known, and the solution may develop steep gradients. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow for the velocity field.

source_provenance.note: Adapted as a natural-language planning task; no numerical data copied.

problem: A researcher studies one-dimensional transport of a scalar quantity with a known constant advection speed. The initial profile is provided, and the spatial domain is periodic. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow to approximate the scalar field over time.

problem: A scientist wants to model a one-dimensional scalar reaction-diffusion process where a concentration diffuses and reacts according to a known nonlinear reaction term. Initial and boundary conditions are available. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow for the concentration field.

problem: An engineer wants to estimate a steady-state potential field in a two-dimensional rectangular region. There are no internal sources, and Dirichlet boundary values are prescribed on all sides. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow for this boundary value problem.

source_provenance.note: Adapted as a natural-language planning task; no numerical data copied.

problem: A researcher wants to approximate the one-dimensional evolution of a scalar concentration governed by both advection and diffusion. The advection speed and diffusion coefficient are known, and boundary and initial data are provided. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow that accounts for both transport and smoothing effects.

source_provenance.note: Adapted as a natural-language planning task; no numerical data copied.

problem: A laboratory measures sparse, possibly noisy temperature values at space-time locations along a one-dimensional rod. The heat equation form is known, but the thermal diffusivity is unknown. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow to infer the diffusivity and reconstruct the temperature field, including any additional physical conditions that must be specified or obtained.

source_provenance.note: Adapted as a natural-language planning task; no numerical data copied.

problem: A researcher observes sparse, possibly noisy velocity measurements from a one-dimensional viscous Burgers system. The governing equation is assumed known, but the viscosity coefficient is unknown. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow to estimate the viscosity and recover the velocity field, including any boundary or initial information that must be specified or obtained.

source_provenance.note: Adapted as a natural-language planning task; no numerical data copied.

problem: A scientist has partial observations of a two-dimensional potential field and wants to infer an unknown source term in a Poisson equation. Boundary information is available, but the source itself must be represented or recovered from data and physics. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow for joint field reconstruction and source recovery.

problem: A team has sparse sensor measurements of a one-dimensional wave field over time. The wave speed is known, but complete initial and boundary information may be unavailable or only partially specified. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow and state what additional information, assumptions, or identifiability checks are required.

problem: A biophysics group observes sparse noisy measurements from a two-species reaction-diffusion process. The coupled reaction form is known, but the observations are limited and noisy. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow that fits both concentration fields while respecting the governing dynamics.

problem: A scientist wants to model a scalar field on a periodic domain governed by advection and diffusion. The initial condition is periodic, and the solution should remain periodic over time. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow that enforces periodicity correctly for a diffusive transport equation.

source_provenance.note: Adapted as a natural-language planning task; no numerical data copied.

problem: A materials scientist wants to approximate phase-separation dynamics governed by an Allen-Cahn-type equation. Initial and boundary information are available, but the solution may develop sharp interfaces and training instability is a concern. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow that addresses stiffness and interface dynamics.

problem: A researcher wants to model a conserved phase-field process governed by a Cahn-Hilliard-type equation. The PDE is fourth order, the dynamics should preserve mass over time, and training high-order derivatives may be difficult. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow for this high-order system.

source_provenance.note: Adapted as a natural-language planning task; no numerical data copied.

problem: A fluid dynamics group wants to approximate a two-dimensional incompressible flow field from partial velocity observations. The model should respect momentum balance and incompressibility, pressure is not directly observed, and boundary or initial conditions must be incorporated when available. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow for velocity and pressure.

source_provenance.note: Adapted as a natural-language planning task; no numerical data copied.

problem: A geoscience team wants to model steady Darcy flow through a heterogeneous porous medium. The spatially varying permeability field is known, and boundary conditions are available. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow to estimate the hydraulic head while correctly accounting for heterogeneous coefficients.

source_provenance.note: Adapted as a natural-language planning task; no numerical data copied.

The solution may be highly oscillatory at high wave numbers. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow and discuss training and validation risks.

The source term and boundary conditions are known, but the solution is highly oscillatory and the geometry contains sharp corners and nontrivial boundaries. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow and discuss why a standard PINN may or may not be sufficient.

problem: An acoustics group wants to model wave scattering in a two-dimensional maze-like domain with internal obstacles. The incident wave, material properties, and boundary descriptions are available, but the geometry is complex and scattered waves interact with many walls. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow that handles geometry, boundary conditions, and scattering-specific validation.

source_provenance.note: Adapted as a natural-language planning stress task; no numerical data copied.

problem: A researcher wants to model a compressible inviscid flow governed by the Euler equations where shocks and contact discontinuities may form from piecewise initial states. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow that respects conservation laws and handles discontinuous solutions without relying only on pointwise smooth residual fitting.

source_provenance.note: Adapted as a natural-language planning stress task; no numerical data copied.

problem: A fluid dynamics group wants to model Rayleigh-Benard convection in a two-dimensional cell heated from below and cooled from above. The workflow must couple velocity, pressure, and temperature fields, respect incompressibility, and capture boundary-layer-driven heat transport. problem: A plasma physics team wants to model a two-dimensional resistive magnetohydrodynamics system with coupled velocity and magnetic fields. The plan must respect momentum balance, magnetic induction, and divergence-free constraints on the velocity and magnetic field. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow that avoids unphysical divergence errors.

pde_system: Multiscale Darcy flow with high-contrast permeability

source_provenance.note: Adapted as a natural-language planning stress task; no numerical data copied.

problem: A geoscience team wants to model Darcy flow across many high-contrast heterogeneous permeability fields. Boundary conditions and source terms are available, and the goal may involve either solving a single difficult instance or learning across a family of coefficient fields. Design a physics-informed modeling workflow and explain when a neural operator, domain decomposition, or coefficient-aware PINN would be appropriate.
